chapter_title.ar: باب الْمَمْلُوكُ يَتَّجِرُ فَيَقَعُ عَلَيْهِ الدَّيْنُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 157 – The owned slave trades, so the debt occurs upon him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/414)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/414)
text: كَانَ أَذِنَ لِغُلَامٍ لَهُ فِي الشِّرَاءِ وَ الْبَيْعِ فَأَفْلَسَ وَ لَزِمَهُ دَيْنٌ فَأُخِذَ بِذَلِكَ الدَّيْنِ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ وَ لَيْسَ يُسَاوِي ثَمَنُهُ مَا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الدَّيْنِ فَسَأَلَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ إِنْ بِعْتَهُ لَزِمَكَ الدَّيْنُ وَ إِنْ أَعْتَقْتَهُ لَمْ يَلْزَمْكَ الدَّيْنُ فَأَعْتَقَهُ فَلَمْ يَلْزَمْهُ شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: One of our companions, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Usman Bin Isa, from Zareyf Al Akfany, said,

‘He authorised a slave of his regarding the buying and the selling, so he became bankrupt, and the debts were necessitated upon him. So he was seized due to that debt which was upon him, and it was not equal to his (slave’s) price what was upon him from the debt. So he asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If you sell him the debts would be necessitated upon you, and if you liberate him, the debts would not be necessitated upon you’. So he liberated him, and nothing was necessitated upon him’.
en.sarwar: 1. Certain persons of our people have narrated Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Zarif al-Akfaniy who has said the following: “This is about the case of a man who authorizes his slave to buy and sell and who becomes bankrupt and is taken by the creditor for the debt which is more than his price. I asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about it. He (the Imam) said, Tf you sell him you are responsible for the debt, but if you set him free you are not responsible for the debt.’ He set him free and he was not held responsible for anything.”
